message:
perf(btree): append-path bypass of CellRef::parse (OPT-A3)

Profile showed CellRef::parse 4.80% self-time on INSERT hot path — the
largest remaining single-symbol cost after OPT-1..7. Root causes found
via perf callgraph on /data/tmp/perf_opts.data:

  - ~2.85% replace_interior_cell defragmentation loop
  - ~0.77% BtCursor::delete defragmentation loops (3 peers)
  - ~0.42% try_table_append_on_hinted_leaf_with_known_last_rowid
            parsing the last cell just to confirm its rowid

Two new helpers in cell.rs:

  - cell::read_table_leaf_rowid_at_offset(page, cell_offset) -> Option<i64>
    Reads the two leading varints (payload_size, rowid) and nothing else.
    Used on the INSERT-append hint-validation path.

  - cell::cell_on_page_size_fast(page, cell_offset, page_type, usable_size)
    Computes the on-page size of a cell directly from its varints,
    skipping the overflow-page PageNumber::new validation done by
    CellRef::parse. Used by the 5 defragmentation loops
    (replace_interior_cell, table-leaf delete, remove_cell_from_leaf,
    separator repair, compact_current_table_leaf).

All five defrag sites and the append-hint rowid check were converted.
On any corruption the helpers return DatabaseCorrupt so callers surface
the same error shape as before. A new cursor unit test
(test_table_try_append_rightmost_leaf_hint_known_last_rowid_rejects_mismatched_hint)
forces the mismatch branch and asserts the cursor falls back to the
generic path with identical end-state to a fresh insert. Eleven new
cell.rs unit tests cross-validate cell_on_page_size_fast against
CellRef::parse + cell_on_page_size across all 4 page types (leaf table
with/without overflow, interior table, leaf index, interior index) and
exercise truncation / out-of-bounds failure modes.

Expected savings: ~3-4% self-time on INSERT append workloads; secondary
wins on DELETE / mixed-DML defrag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
